note: UniswapX order cancellation = on-chain Permit2 invalidateUnorderedNonces(uint256 wordPos,uint256 mask) (selector 0x3ff9dcb1) against canonical Permit2 — UniswapX orders use Permit2 SignatureTransfer UNORDERED nonces, so cancel flips bits in nonceBitmap[swapper][wordPos], reverting the order's permitWitnessTransferFrom (research §3.4). This is CALLDATA (not a typed-data signature). MODELING APPROXIMATIONS: (1) cancel is Permit2-nonce-word granular, NOT per-order — one invalidateUnorderedNonces invalidates a whole 256-nonce word, so venue.reactor=$to (canonical Permit2, a venue-agnostic placeholder — not a specific UniswapX reactor) and order_hash=$args.wordPos (the invalidated nonce WORD, not a real 32-byte order hash); (2) signature omitted (None) — this is an on-chain tx, not a signed cancel. CancelIntentOrderAction.order_hash is typed String so the decimal wordPos serializes directly. Chains where UniswapX reactors deploy: mainnet/base/optimism/arbitrum.
